text: Fill in the blanks — General Features of the Cardiovascular System
questions: The three layers of a blood vessel wall are tunica *intima*, tunica *media*, and tunica *adventitia*.
Elastic arteries exhibit the *Windkessel* function — they stretch during systole and *recoil* during diastole to maintain continuous blood flow.
Arterioles are called *resistance* vessels because they are the main determinants of peripheral vascular resistance.
An *end* artery has no anastomoses, so its blockage leads to *infarction/ischaemic necrosis* of the tissue it supplies.
In the fetal circulation, the *ductus arteriosus* shunts blood from the pulmonary trunk to the aorta, bypassing the non-functional *lungs*.
